# X2Chess Architecture Manual

Last updated: 2026-04-03

## 1) Goals

- Make the browser fat client the primary/full product runtime.
- Allow remote launch (server-hosted assets) while preserving local client execution.
- Combine local resource usage with remote resource integration in one client architecture.
- Provide an app-profile variant as a reduced version of the full fat client.
- Keep architecture decisions explicit and traceable.

Architecture decisions are maintained in `doc/architecture-adr-manual.qmd`.

## 2) System Overview

## 2.1 Frontend (`frontend/`)

- Technology: Vite + React 18 + TypeScript (ES modules).
- Main entrypoint: `frontend/src/main.tsx`.
- React state: single `useReducer` store (`AppStoreState`) owned by `AppProvider`; all mutations via `dispatch(action)`.
- Service layer: `useAppStartup` hook initialises all pure-logic services on mount and exposes stable callbacks via `ServiceContext`; components consume them with `useServiceContext()`.
- Responsibilities:
  - Render the Game-Viewer (board + PGN editor UI) as React components.
  - Parse PGN into an internal model and serialize back to text.
  - Keep replay/navigation state in React `useReducer`.
  - Manage chess move playback and UI interactions via `ServiceContext` callbacks.
  - Manage multiple open game sessions in parallel tabs.
  - Route load/save through a uniform source adapter contract.

## 2.2 Backend (`backend/`)

- Technology: FastAPI.
- Scope in current architecture:
  - Optional static serving and optional future remote services.
  - Not part of client-local resource loading/saving.
- Constraint:
  - Local runtime resources are owned and handled only by the client runtime.

## 2.3 App Profile Shell (`frontend/src-tauri/`)

- Technology: Tauri 2 (Rust host + web frontend).
- Configuration: `frontend/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`.
- Runtime entrypoint: `frontend/src-tauri/src/main.rs`.
- Capability model:
  - `frontend/src-tauri/capabilities/default.json` with `core:default`.
- Build wiring:
  - Dev URL `http://localhost:5287`.
  - Production frontend assets from `frontend/dist`.

## 2.4 Resource Library (`resource/`)

- Technology: TypeScript library consumed by frontend runtime boundaries.
- Scope in current architecture:
  - Defines canonical PGN resource contracts (`PgnResourceRef`, `PgnGameRef`, `PgnGameEntry`).
  - Provides canonical kind model: `file`, `directory`, `db`.
  - Owns metadata schema/constants and extraction contracts.
  - Owns database schema and migrations under `resource/database/`.
- Boundary constraints:
  - Frontend modules consume `resource/client` and `resource/domain` contracts.
  - Frontend must not own DB schema/migration internals.

## 3) Build and Delivery Paths

Environment and toolchain setup is documented in `doc/setup-manual.qmd`.

## 3.1 Full browser fat-client profile
- Dev (local): `cd frontend && npm run dev`
- Build (deployable from remote host or local static host): `cd frontend && npm run build`

## 3.2 Reduced app profile (Tauri)
- Dev shell + hot reload: `cd frontend && npm run desktop:dev`
- Packaged app profile: `cd frontend && npm run desktop:pack`
- Artifact directory: `frontend/src-tauri/target/release/bundle/`

## 3.3 Runtime modes and Developer-Tools

- Build mode is injected as `__X2CHESS_MODE__` (`DEV` or `PROD`).
- Default behavior:
  - `DEV`: Developer-Tools starts ON.
  - `PROD`: Developer-Tools starts OFF.
- Developer-Tools can be toggled in the app menu and persists per user.
- Developer-Tools exposes a bottom fixed dock with tabs:
  - `AST`
  - `DOM`
  - `Raw PGN`
- The dock is user-opened and user-closed; enabling Developer-Tools does not force the dock open.
- Dock views always reflect the same in-memory game/model state as the main Game-Viewer.
- DEV-only convenience:
  - Automatic preload attempt from `run/DEV/games` (when available).

## 3.4 Game sessions and source adapters

- Open games are modeled as in-memory `GameSession` entries:
  - `sessionId`
  - `sourceRef`
  - `revisionToken`
  - snapshot of editor/board runtime
  - dirty/save mode metadata
- Session identity is independent from source identity; source references are attached metadata.
- Source interactions are routed through canonical resource boundaries:
  - `file` kind: single file containing multiple games
  - `directory` kind: folder of one-game-per-file entries
  - `db` kind: database-backed resources (schema/migrations owned in `resource/database/`; implementation may be deferred by profile)
- Supported open flows are:
  - Drag/drop PGN text/files
  - Paste PGN text
  - Source-root preload (DEV convenience)
- Save path is uniform:
  - Active session + source reference + revision token -> source adapter `save(...)`.
- Save policy:
  - Default mode is autosave.
  - User can switch active-session mode between autosave and manual from the app menu.
- Naming convention:
  - The conjunction of board and text editor is called **Game-Viewer**.

## 3.5 App-Version and Data-Migration Strategy

All persistent data in X2Chess is versioned so that older instances can be detected and
upgraded automatically on startup. Three layers are covered:

### 3.5.1 App version constant

- **Source of truth:** `frontend/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json` → injected as `__X2CHESS_APP_VERSION__` by Vite.
- **Access point:** `frontend/src/runtime/app_version.ts` exports `CURRENT_APP_VERSION: string`,
  `parseSemver`, and `isNewerVersion`. All code that needs the version reads from this module;
  no other file duplicates the inline `typeof __X2CHESS_APP_VERSION__` guard.

### 3.5.2 SQLite database schema migrations

- **Owner:** `resource/database/migrations/` (runner + steps + manifest).
- **Mechanism:** `schema_version` table records applied versions. `runMigrations(db)` is idempotent —
  safe to call on every DB open; it skips already-applied steps.
- **Naming convention:** migration files are prefixed `NNNN_` (e.g. `0001_init.ts`).
- **Rule:** Never modify a migration step after it has been shipped. Add a new step instead.

### 3.5.3 localStorage versioned stores

- **Primitive:** `frontend/src/storage/versioned_store.ts` — `createVersionedStore<T>(config)`.
- **Envelope format:** every managed key stores `{ "v": <number>, "data": <T> }` JSON.
- **On read:**
  - Absent key → return default (no write-back).
  - Current version → return data directly.
  - Older version → apply migration chain in-place; write upgraded envelope back.
  - Future version (data from a newer build) → return default *without overwriting*.
  - Corrupt JSON → reset key to default; log a warning.
- **Adding a store:** call `createVersionedStore` with `version: 1` and `migrations: []`.
  Increment `version` and add a migration step whenever the stored shape changes incompatibly.
- **Migration step indexing:** `migrations[i]` upgrades data from version `i` to version `i+1`.
  Array length must equal `version - 1`. A step returning `null` signals unrecoverable data
  and triggers a reset to `defaultValue`.

#### Managed stores (version 1 baseline)

| Store | Key | File |
|---|---|---|
| Shell prefs (compound) | `x2chess.shellPrefs` | `runtime/shell_prefs_store.ts` |
| Shape / board decoration prefs | `x2chess.shapePrefs.v1` | `runtime/shape_prefs.ts` |
| Resource viewer column prefs | `x2chess.resourceViewerColumnPrefs.v1` | `resources_viewer/viewer_utils.ts` |
| Metadata schemas | `x2chess.metadata-schemas` | `resources_viewer/schema_storage.ts` |
| External DB settings | `x2chess.ext-db-settings` | `runtime/ext_database_settings_store.ts` |
| Remote rules cache | `x2chess.webImportRules` | `runtime/remote_rules_store.ts` |
| User web-import rules | `x2chess.webImport.userRules` | `resources/web_import/user_rules_storage.ts` |
| Training badges | `x2chess.training-badges` | `training/transcript_storage.ts` |
| Training sessions | `x2chess.training-sessions` | `training/transcript_storage.ts` |
| Curriculum plan | `x2chess.curriculum-plan` | `training/curriculum/curriculum_storage.ts` |

Dynamic per-tab group-by state (`x2chess.groupby.<tabId>`) and the visual-asset cache
(`chess-app:visual-asset:v2:<key>`) are intentionally excluded — they are ephemeral UI state
and asset caches respectively, not user configuration.

### 3.5.4 Legacy key consolidation (one-shot migration)

Individual shell-preference keys written by earlier builds (`x2chess.sound`,
`x2chess.moveDelayMs`, `x2chess.locale`, `x2chess.pgnLayout`, `x2chess.developerTools`,
`x2chess.positionPreviewOnHover`, `x2chess.resourceViewerHeightPx`,
`x2chess.boardColumnWidthPx`) are merged into the compound `x2chess.shellPrefs` key on
the **first startup after upgrade** by `migrateLocalStorage()` in
`frontend/src/storage/migrate_local_storage.ts`. The function is idempotent: if the
compound key already exists it returns immediately. Legacy keys are deleted after consolidation.

Similarly, `migrateRemoteRulesCache()` in `runtime/remote_rules_store.ts` merges the
legacy two-key remote rules cache (`x2chess.webImportRules.v1` +
`x2chess.webImportRules.version`) into the compound `x2chess.webImportRules` key.

### 3.5.5 Startup sequence

```
useAppStartup mount effect
  │
  ├─ [SYNC] migrateLocalStorage()         ← consolidate legacy shell-pref keys
  ├─ [SYNC] migrateRemoteRulesCache()     ← consolidate legacy rules-cache keys
  ├─ [SYNC] versioned store reads         ← auto-migrate on first access
  │
  └─ [ASYNC, inside Tauri gateway open]
       runMigrations(db)                  ← idempotent DB schema migration
```

### 3.5.6 Version-gating (future)

Neither localStorage nor DB migrations currently require app-version gating (all steps
are additive). If a future migration must only run on a specific version transition, use
`CURRENT_APP_VERSION` / `parseSemver` from `runtime/app_version.ts` as the guard inside
the migration step or `migrateLocalStorage`.

## 4) Configuration

Configuration in X2Chess splits into two distinct categories: **build-time configuration**
embedded at compile time and **user preferences** persisted at runtime.

### 4.1 Build-time configuration

Build-time values are fixed when the application is compiled. They cannot be changed at runtime.

**Source of truth:** `frontend/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`

This file owns:

- `version` — the application semver string (canonical source for `CURRENT_APP_VERSION`;
  see §3.5.1).
- `productName`, `identifier` — product and bundle identity.
- Window dimensions and minimum constraints (`width`, `height`, `minWidth`, `minHeight`).
- Updater endpoint and signing public key.

**Vite build globals**

Vite reads `tauri.conf.json` at build time (`frontend/vite.config.ts`) and injects three
compile-time constants into every bundle:

| Global | Value | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `__X2CHESS_MODE__` | `"DEV"` or `"PROD"` | Controls developer-tools default; consumed by `runtime/bootstrap_prefs.ts`. |
| `__X2CHESS_APP_VERSION__` | Semver string from `tauri.conf.json` | Re-exported exclusively from `runtime/app_version.ts`; used for version-gated migrations. |
| `__X2CHESS_BUILD_TIMESTAMP__` | ISO-8601 string set at build time | Available for diagnostic display. |

All code that needs the version reads it from `runtime/app_version.ts`. No other file reads
`__X2CHESS_APP_VERSION__` directly.

### 4.2 User preferences

User preferences are values the user can change within the application. They persist across
sessions via the versioned localStorage mechanism described in §3.5.3.

Each preference group is a named versioned store backed by a single localStorage key.
Stores are read once on startup (after legacy key consolidation; see §3.5.5) and written
back whenever a preference changes.

#### Preference stores

| Store | Key | File | Contents |
|---|---|---|---|
| Shell prefs | `x2chess.shellPrefs` | `runtime/shell_prefs_store.ts` | Sound, locale, move delay, PGN layout mode, position preview, developer tools, panel sizes |
| Shape / board prefs | `x2chess.shapePrefs.v1` | `runtime/shape_prefs.ts` | Annotation colors, square highlight style, move-hint dots |
| External DB settings | `x2chess.ext-db-settings` | `runtime/ext_database_settings_store.ts` | Opening explorer speed/rating filters |
| Resource viewer column prefs | `x2chess.resourceViewerColumnPrefs.v1` | `resources_viewer/viewer_utils.ts` | Column widths and visibility |

For the complete list of all managed stores see the table in §3.5.3.

#### Preference lifecycle

1. **Startup** — `useAppStartup` reads all stores synchronously; the values initialise
   `AppStoreState` and service-layer state via `syncStateToReact()`.
2. **Change** — a user action dispatches through `ServiceContext`. The setter writes the
   store synchronously (`store.write(value)`) and updates the service-layer state.
   Shell-pref setters dispatch directly to React and bypass `render()` (see §6.3).
3. **Future build** — if a newer build already wrote a higher-versioned envelope, the
   current build returns `defaultValue` without overwriting. The newer data is preserved
   for when that build runs again.

#### Adding a preference

1. Add the field to the relevant `*Prefs` type and to `DEFAULT_*_PREFS`.
2. If the stored shape changes incompatibly, increment `version` and append a
   `MigrationStep` to `migrations`.
3. Expose a setter callback in `ServiceContext` and wire it in `createAppServices.ts`.
4. Register the store in the table in §3.5.3 of this manual.

## 5) Testing

### 5.1 Test runner and location

Tests use Node's built-in test runner (`node:test`) invoked through `tsx`
for TypeScript support.  No third-party test framework or mocking library is
used; tests rely on real implementations or hand-written stubs.

```bash
# from frontend/
npm test                                       # run all tests
tsx --test "test/foo/**/*.test.ts"             # run a subset
```

Test files live under `frontend/test/`, mirroring the `src/` structure
exactly — for example `src/board/shape_serializer.ts` →
`test/board/shape_serializer.test.ts`.

### 5.2 What to test

**Pure-logic modules are the primary test target.** Any module under `model/`,
`editor/`, `board/`, `game_sessions/`, `resources/`, `resources_viewer/`,
`runtime/`, or `training/` that exports non-trivial logic should have a
corresponding test file.

The `state/app_reducer.ts` reducer and `state/selectors.ts` are pure functions
and are tested like any other pure-logic module, despite living in `state/`.

**React components, hooks, and Tauri gateways are not unit-tested.** They
belong to the integration layer and are validated through manual QA.

### 5.3 Coverage map

| Test directory | Source coverage |
|---|---|
| `test/board/` | `src/board/` — move lookup, navigation, shape parser/serializer, physical board protocols |
| `test/editor/` | `src/editor/` — FEN utils, tree numbering, collapse, editor plan, anchor resolver |
| `test/engines/` | Engine manager, UCI parser/writer/session |
| `test/game_sessions/` | Session store, persistence, ingress handlers |
| `test/model/` | PGN model parser, serializer, commands, move ops, undo/redo, x2-style |
| `test/resources/` | Material key, PGN DB adapter, PGN metadata, web-import rules |
| `test/resources_viewer/` | Annotation parsers: anchor, eval, link, QA, schema storage, todo, train tag |
| `test/runtime/` | Resource ref utilities |
| `test/state/` | App reducer, selectors |
| `test/training/` | Move acceptance, replay protocol, transcript, merge, train-tag parser, curriculum I/O |

### 5.4 Writing new tests

- Import source files with the `.js` extension (required by the ESM/tsx loader):
  `import { foo } from "../../src/bar.js"`.
- Use `String.raw\`…\`` for strings containing backslash sequences (PGN escape
  characters, regex literals) to avoid double-escaping noise.
- Stub external dependencies with minimal inline objects; never duplicate
  production logic in stubs.
- One test file per source module — do not mix multiple modules into one file.

## 6) Key Concepts

### 6.1 GameSessionState and ActiveSessionRef

Each open game tab is backed by a dedicated `GameSessionState` object
(`frontend/src/game_sessions/game_session_state.ts`). This object is the
single source of truth for all game-specific mutable state for one session:

- **PGN / model**: `pgnModel`, `pgnText`, `moves`, `verboseMoves`,
  `movePositionById`, `pgnLayoutMode`
- **Navigation**: `currentPly`, `selectedMoveId`, `boardPreview`,
  `animationRunId`, `isAnimating`
- **Editor messages**: `errorMessage`, `statusMessage`, `pendingFocusCommentId`
- **Edit history**: `undoStack`, `redoStack`

Service modules (`pgn_runtime`, `history`, `move_lookup`, `navigation`,
`pgn_model_update`) never receive a global state object. They receive an
`ActiveSessionRef = { current: GameSessionState }` and read/write only
`sessionRef.current.*`. This makes the active session swappable without
copying data: a session switch is a single pointer assignment
`activeSessionRef.current = target.ownState`. Each `GameSession` in the
session store holds its live `GameSessionState` in an `ownState` field —
there is no freeze/thaw snapshot cycle.

### 6.2 Shared Application State

Non-game state lives in `AppState` (`app_shell/app_state.ts`): resource
viewer tabs, player store, file system handles, save mode defaults, app
config, and a few service-read fields (locale, sound, moveDelayMs). This is
a shared mutable object referenced directly by service modules. It holds no
game-specific fields.

Shell preferences (sound, locale, move delay, layout mode, position preview,
developer tools) are the authoritative province of the React store
(`AppStoreState`) and are persisted via `shellPrefsStore` — a versioned
localStorage store (`frontend/src/runtime/shell_prefs_store.ts`). Shell
preference setters dispatch to React directly and do not go through
`render()`.

### 6.3 The React State Bridge

The React `useReducer` store (`AppStoreState` / `appReducer`) is the
authoritative state for all rendering. The bridge between the service layer
and React is the `render()` function assembled in `createAppServices.ts`:

1. Any service mutation calls `render()` (or `syncStateToReact()` in
   `useAppStartup`).
2. `render()` reads `activeSessionRef.current` (game state) and `sharedState`
   (shared state), then dispatches typed actions to the React store.
3. React components read state exclusively through selectors
   (`state/selectors.ts`).
4. Components trigger mutations exclusively via `ServiceContext` callbacks —
   never `dispatch` for game logic.

Shell preference setters are the only exception: they dispatch to React
directly and bypass `render()`.

## 7) Constraints for Ongoing Work

- Keep browser fat-client as primary source of feature truth.
- Keep reduced app-profile differences explicit and documented.
- Keep Tauri as reduced app shell, not a replacement for frontend domain logic.
- Keep local resource APIs explicit and maintainable.
- Keep local resource read/write in client code only (no backend mediation).
- Keep remote integrations behind adapter boundaries and contracts.
- Keep top-level `resource/` as canonical home for PGN resource contracts, kind routing, and metadata schema.
- Keep canonical resource kinds explicit as `file`, `directory`, and `db`.
- Keep SQL schema/migration ownership in `resource/database/` (never under frontend domain modules).
- Keep backend optional and decoupled from local resource workflows.
- Enforce Component-Contract on components (integration + configuration + communication APIs, documented publicly in code).
- Enforce clean/professional CSS defaults and maintain explicit theming extension points for future user adaptation.
- Keep local runtime data scenario-separated under `run/`.
- Keep production runtime data outside repository paths (explicit prod root or OS app-data default).
- Keep scenario game libraries under `run/<SCENARIO>/games` for local scenarios.
- Keep runtime configuration layered: default app config + partial user override in data area.
- Keep all UI state in the React `useReducer` store (`AppStoreState`); never mutate state outside `dispatch`.
- Keep service initialisation in `useAppStartup`; expose service callbacks exclusively via `ServiceContext`.
- Pure-logic modules (`model/`, `editor/`, `board/`, `game_sessions/`, `resources/`) must remain framework-free.
- Update this manual when architecture decisions, boundaries, or delivery paths change.
